title: What a Valid P≠NP Proof Must Look Like

analysis.object: T910: What a Valid P≠NP Proof Must Look Like depth analysis

needs_eml3.description: If EML-2 methods cant cross the boundary, the proof must use EML-3 (oscillatory, spectral, representation-theoretic).

cross_type_methods.description: Cross-type: combine EML-2 (algebraic) and EML-3 (spectral). GCT does exactly this: GL(n) representations (EML-3) applied to circuit complexity (EML-2).

gct_structure.description: GCT: Mulmuley uses representation theory (EML-3) to prove that perm cannot be a projection of det. The permanent is EML-inf; det is EML-2. Proving they differ = depth separation.
gct_structure.reason: GCT uses EML-3 to prove EML-2 vs EML-inf

spectral_methods.description: Spectral graph theory (EML-2/EML-3 boundary). Expander graphs. Fourier analysis on Boolean functions. These are EML-3 operations on EML-2 objects.

untried_approaches.description: Cross-type approaches unexplored: tropical-spectral hybrid (T297 + spectral theory); EML-3 shadow lifting; LUC instance for P≠NP.

depth_crossing_required.description: A valid P≠NP proof must achieve Δd=+∞ (crossing EML-2 to EML-inf). Only EML-3 methods can approach EML-inf. The proof must include an EML-3 spine.

t910_theorem.description: T910: A valid P≠NP proof requires EML-3 or cross-type methods. EML-2 methods are ruled out by T909. The proof must use spectral, representation-theoretic, or oscillatory techniques to achieve the Δd=+inf crossing. T910.
t910_theorem.reason: Valid proof structure: EML-3 spine required

analysis.theorem: T910: What a Valid P≠NP Proof Must Look Like (S1190).

key_theorem: T910: What a Valid P≠NP Proof Must Look Like (S1190).
